title: 10 best herbal creams for emphysema

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with emphysema because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce lung inflammation and scarring. The creams' active ingredients, such as Ginkgo biloba and Maidenhair fern extract, have been shown to improve lung function by increasing oxygen absorption and reducing airway constriction. By targeting the underlying causes of emphysema, these creams can help to slow disease progression and improve respiratory symptoms, ultimately enhancing quality of life for individuals affected by this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal antioxidants like curcumin (found in turmeric) or green tea extract can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which may help slow down the progression of emphysema.

Bronchodilators: Certain herbal extracts like ginseng or ephedra can help relax airway smooth muscle, making it easier to breathe by increasing airflow and relieving symptoms of bronchospasm associated with emphysema.

Inflammation modulators: Herbal anti-inflammatory compounds like boswellia (found in frankincense) or feverfew may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and alleviate symptoms of emphysema, such as coughing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds like ginkgolides and bilobalide, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, making them potentially useful for treating emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 100 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves, washing them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or by using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba powder with 100 milliliters of organic sweet almond oil and 50 millilitres of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 60°c for 30 minutes, then allow it to cool.
Whip the cooled herbal mixture with an immersion blender or a hand whisk for 2-3 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with emphysema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the lungs. The curcumin present in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating symptoms such as coughing and shortness of breath. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and promoting lung health, turmeric creams may help improve quality of life for individuals suffering from emphysema.
remedy_properties: Cytokine modulation: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation by modulating cytokines, which are proteins that promote inflammation in emphysema, leading to lung damage.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in turmeric creams, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in emphysema-prone lungs.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly curcumin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs by inhibiting enzymes like cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), which contributes to chronic inflammation in emphysema.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain high amounts of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate symptoms of emphysema such as coughing and shortness of breath.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like emphysema due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric rhizomes weighing approximately 500 grams.
Dry the turmeric in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 200 grams of dried turmeric into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the turmeric powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and mixed thoroughly.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with emphysema because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works to reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a common symptom of emphysema. Additionally, licorice creams also help to increase mucus clearance, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and other debris from the lungs. By reducing inflammation and improving mucus clearance, herbal licorice creams may help alleviate symptoms of emphysema and improve overall lung function.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, common characteristics of emphysema.
Licoric acid: This flavonoid has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe for individuals with emphysema.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has been found to have anti-fibrotic effects, which may help reduce scarring in the lungs caused by emphysema, improving lung function and overall respiratory health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help reduce bronchial constriction in patients with emphysema.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes are rich in flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate symptoms of emphysema such as shortness of breath and coughing.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 10% w/w glycyrrhizin powder, 80% w/w coconut oil, and 10% w/w beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 15 minutes to reach a temperature of 180°f.
Add the glycyrrhizin powder to the melted coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Gradually add the beeswax to the mixture, stirring constantly for 10 minutes until it reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Remove from heat and allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with emphysema because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective gel-like substance that soothes and lubricates the airways. This natural coating helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the lungs, making it easier for people with emphysema to breathe. Additionally, marshmallow creams can help to calm coughs and congestion, providing relief from symptoms associated with this chronic lung disease. As a result, many people find that using herbal marshmallow creams as part of their treatment plan helps them manage their condition more effectively.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by emphysema.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and alleviate symptoms of emphysema such as coughing and shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: The mucilage in marshmallow root also acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with emphysema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm bronchial passages, making them ideal for relieving emphysema symptoms.
Barks: Containing saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways associated with emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis to obtain one cup of dried herb material.
Combine the dried marshmallow root with two cups of distilled water in a heat-proof container and steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids retaining the liquid extract.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax with three tablespoons of coconut oil and five tablespoons of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Gradually add the marshmallow root liquid extract to the oil mixture while stirring constantly until a smooth emulsified cream forms within 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with emphysema because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the lungs. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation is particularly effective in alleviating symptoms of emphysema, such as chronic coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. By reducing inflammation, the cream also helps to open up airways and improve lung function, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall respiratory health. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may also help slow down the progression of emphysema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a common complication of emphysema.

Mucus-thinning properties: The cream's mucilage content helps to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel and reducing congestion and coughing associated with emphysema.
Antioxidant properties: Stinging nettle cream's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals in the lungs, which can damage lung tissue and exacerbate symptoms of emphysema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues associated with emphysema.
Buds: High in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C, which has antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality stinging nettle leaves and dry them using desiccators or air circulation for 7 days.
Combine dried nettle leaves with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:2 and infuse mixture at room temperature for 6 hours.
Filter infused liquid through cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids and transfer filtered liquid to a double boiler.
Add beeswax and shea butter to the filtered liquid in a ratio of 1:1 and heat mixture in double boiler until it reaches 180°f for 20 minutes.
Remove cream from heat, let it cool slightly, and pour it into sterilized containers while maintaining a consistency of 40-50% oil content.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound creams helps with emphysema because they contain marshmallow root, which soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the lungs. This allows for easier breathing and reduces inflammation caused by chronic bronchitis, a common complication of emphysema. The anti-inflammatory properties of horehound also help to reduce mucus production, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion. As a result, herbal horehound creams can provide natural relief from the symptoms of emphysema, improving overall respiratory health and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Horehound cream's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with emphysema.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in horehound cream may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to the progression of emphysema.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Horehound's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, which is a common characteristic of emphysema, and alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Horehound roots contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which help to soothe and calm respiratory issues associated with emphysema.
Barks: The barks of the horehound plant contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the lungs and airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marrubium vulgare leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Chop 20 grams of freshly harvested leaves and steep them in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discard solids.
Mix equal parts of the cooled herbal liquid with sweet almond oil and beeswax, stir until wax melts completely.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly and label as needed, store in refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with emphysema because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a hallmark of emphysema. By decreasing inflammation, ginger creams may help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with emphysema. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of ginger creams may also help slow down lung function decline, making them a potential adjunctive therapy for managing this chronic respiratory condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and alleviate symptoms of emphysema, such as wheezing and coughing.

Expectorant properties: The natural oils and extracts present in herbal ginger creams have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant compounds can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of emphysema and other respiratory diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of emphysema such as bronchial constriction and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the ginger plant contain compounds that have been shown to improve lung function and reduce oxidative stress in patients with emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried zingiber officinale root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered ginger with 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the ginger's active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil, heating gently over low heat until melted and well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with emphysema because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called parthenolide, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs. Emphysema is characterized by chronic inflammation and damage to the alveoli, the tiny air sacs where oxygen exchange occurs. By reducing inflammation, feverfew creams may help slow down lung damage and improve breathing function in individuals with emphysema. Additionally, feverfew's ability to relax bronchial muscles may also help ease shortness of breath and other symptoms associated with the condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Feverfew's ability to reduce inflammation can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath associated with respiratory conditions like bronchitis.
Antispasmodic properties: The spasm-relieving effects of feverfew can help ease bronchospasms, which are common in emphysema patients, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Antioxidant properties: Feverfew's antioxidant activity may help protect against oxidative stress and lung damage caused by free radicals, which is a potential contributing factor to the progression of emphysema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots have been traditionally used as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent.
Barks: The bark of the feverfew plant contains sesquiterpenes, which are believed to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of tanacetum parthenium in amounts of 20 grams for subsequent processing.
Combine harvested tanacetum parthenium flowers with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:10 and steep mixture for 2 weeks.
Strain infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids after 24 hours.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the strained oil in quantities of 5% and 0.5% respectively before whipping until thickened.
Pour whipped mixture into sterilized tubes or containers for immediate use or storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with emphysema because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce lung inflammation, a common symptom of the disease. The omega-6 fatty acids present in borage oil also help to improve lung function and promote healing by reducing oxidative stress. Additionally, borage creams may help to soothe bronchial passages and reduce coughing, which can further alleviate symptoms associated with emphysema. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal borage creams offer a natural and effective approach to managing the condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Borage cream's omega-6 fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and alleviate symptoms of emphysema.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in borage cream, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage lung tissues and contribute to emphysema progression.

Immune-modulating properties: GLA in borage cream has been shown to modulate the immune response, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, thus slowing down emphysema's progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with emphysema.
Seeds: Containing omega-6 fatty acids, the seeds provide anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried borago officinalis flowers from a reliable source.
Combine the dried flowers with 40 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-proof glass container.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes to allow for gentle extraction and infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil then heat at low temperature for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with emphysema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and bronchial passages. The cream's antioxidants also work to combat oxidative stress, a known contributor to emphysema progression. Additionally, sage's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits. By addressing these underlying issues, sage creams may help alleviate symptoms of emphysema and improve overall lung function.

Expectorant properties: Sage's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with emphysema.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal sage creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a hallmark of emphysema and can contribute to its progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with emphysema.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds that help to relax airway smooth muscle, improving lung function and reducing symptoms of emphysema.
Barks: Possess flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in late summer and dry them for at least two weeks to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler, then add the powdered salvia officinalis and stir until fully incorporated for five minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for two hours before whipping it with an electric mixer for three minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to six months or refrigerate for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with emphysema because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the lungs, a primary characteristic of the disease. The cream's antioxidant compounds also help to combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate lung damage. Additionally, thyme's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Expectorant properties: Thyme's natural expectorants help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion and coughing associated with emphysema.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can improve lung function and slow down the progression of emphysema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with emphysema.
Rhyzomes: Thyme rhyzomes have been shown to possess antibacterial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that exacerbate emphysema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried thyme leaves from the plant thymus vulgaris.
Mix the dried thyme leaves with 20 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature of 60 degrees celsius for one hour using a double boiler or a thermometer-controlled heating device.
Allow the mixture to cool and then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Add 10 milliliters of beeswax to the infused oil and stir until melted then pour into sterilized containers for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with emphysema because it contains a unique blend of herbal extracts that work synergistically to reduce inflammation in the lungs. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm and soothe the airways, making breathing easier for individuals suffering from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Additionally, the cream's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and debris, further reducing symptoms of emphysema.

Barks: Containing a unique blend of terpenes and sesquiterpenes, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including chronic bronchitis and emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried eucalyptus leaves and 1 cup of coconut oil to begin preparation.
Infuse the eucalyptus leaves in hot water for 20 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the infusion and combine it with melted coconut oil, beeswax, and 10 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and heat it gently until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the consistency is smooth.
Pour the eucalyptus globulus cream into a sterile container and let it cool before use after at least 30 minutes of preparation time.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and expectorant: Eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve congestion and coughs associated with emphysema.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, a common symptom of emphysema.
Antimicrobial: Eucalyptus oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate respiratory conditions like emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with emphysema because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavender flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the lavender powder with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to create a smooth and consistent texture.
Remove from heat and let it cool slightly before pouring into sterilized glass jars and labeling properly.
Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with emphysema because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and relax the airways, reducing inflammation and swelling. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them completely using a clean dryer or air circulation method.
Combine 20 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 100 ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and let it infuse for 4 hours under room temperature.
Filter the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids and obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 50 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of shea butter, and 20 ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized containers, let it cool and solidify before storing at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with emphysema because it provides a soothing and protective barrier for the lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by chronic bronchitis. The mucilage in slippery elm binds to irritated lung tissue, coating and calming the airways, making it easier to breathe. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ulmus rubra bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for at least 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered ulmus rubra bark with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant bowl at a ratio of 3:2:1.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 45-60 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken for about 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months or until used.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in elder cream, including elderberry and other botanicals, have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, a hallmark of emphysema.

Mucus-thinning properties: Elder cream's expectorant properties, such as those from eucalyptus and thyme, can help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms like coughing and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, making them an effective anti-inflammatory component in creams for emphysema treatment.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain triterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve lung function in people with chronic respiratory diseases like emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to your local regulations.
Combine one part sambucus nigra herb with two parts coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 160°f to extract the bioactive compounds into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with emphysema because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the lungs, thereby alleviating symptoms such as chronic coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. The cream's antioxidants also help to protect the lungs from further damage caused by oxidative stress, allowing the body to repair itself more efficiently. Additionally, the creams' soothing properties can help to calm irritated airways, making it easier to breathe and improving overall lung function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of emphysema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams neutralize free radicals that damage lung tissue, preventing further deterioration of lung function.
Mucolytic properties: Pot marigold's mucolytic compounds help break down and thin mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory compounds found in the roots help reduce inflammation in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of emphysema.
Barks: The bark of the pot marigold plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe and protect the airways, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak blooming stage.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven for 4 hours.
Combine 100g of dried calendula flowers with 300ml of coconut oil and simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discard solids and store in fridge.
Allow the cream to infuse for at least 24 hours before use and shake well before applying.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with emphysema because of its natural ability to ease breathing difficulties. The menthol present in peppermint has a bronchodilating effect, which relaxes the airways and increases lung capacity. This allows for easier airflow and reduces wheezing and coughing. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for emphysema patients to breathe more comfortably.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Peppermint's menthol content can help loosen mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel, which may provide temporary relief for respiratory issues like coughs and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the airways, potentially easing symptoms of respiratory conditions like bronchitis or asthma.
Bronchodilatory effect: Peppermint's menthol content can also help relax bronchial smooth muscle, increasing airflow to the lungs and making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help to soothe and calm airway inflammation associated with emphysema.
Rhyzomes: Containing volatile oils like menthol and menthone, peppermint rhyzomes have been traditionally used to ease bronchial constriction and promote expectoration in patients with emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Blend 20g of dried peppermint powder with 30ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 70°c over low heat while stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard solids.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30-60 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for emphysema are topical treatments that harness the natural healing properties of plants to alleviate the symptoms of emphysema. These creams help to soothe and relax the airways, reducing inflammation and improving breathing. Examples of herbal creams that have shown promise in treating emphysema include those containing marshmallow root, slippery elm, and lobelia inflata. By using these creams, individuals with emphysema can experience improved lung function, reduced coughing, and increased energy levels, ultimately enhancing their quality of life and allowing them to enjoy activities they previously found challenging due to their condition.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat emphysema with herbal creams is to look for natural ingredients that soothe and calm bronchial inflammation. Ginkgo biloba and St. John's Wort are two herbs known to help reduce inflammation and improve oxygen flow to the lungs, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Apply a cream containing these herbs topically to the chest area 2-3 times daily for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to emphysema that are treated with herbal creams are chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), bronchitis, and asthma. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint can help soothe and ease respiratory issues by reducing inflammation and opening up airways. These creams may also contain anti-oxidants to combat free radicals and promote healthy lung function.
